notes: First physical X-wing concept; important precursor but not the ILM filming family.
First completed ILM X-wing; sent to England as Blue 1 and later finished as Red 2.
One of the four bespoke ANH hero X-wings.
Separate bespoke hero build, distinct from the pyro Red 1 later dressed as Red 3.
Hero Red 5 was later photographed with one stripe greyed over to read as Red 4; this should not be treated as a separate ANH hero Red 4 build.
ILM states there was a fifth unfinished hero example that appears to have stayed with the shop through the OT period.
Best-documented pyro lineage: a Red Leader pyro that survived its engine-hit shot and was later dressed as Red 3 for possible later work.
Positively listed by ILM among identified pyros, but with less exposed surviving lineage than XW-P1.
ILM states Red 6 seems to have been detonated and later donated parts to become Red 10.
Listed by ILM among identified pyros; exact physical survival chain remains less visible.
Auction material confirms additional lower-quality, gray-speckled X-wing pyros were built when more destruction models were needed than originally planned.
Useful design origin, not the final ILM filming family.

common_labels: Red Jammer; Red Y-Wing; first ILM Y-Wing
notes: First ILM Y-wing from December 1975, sent incomplete to England for set-reference work and later finished.
Heritage identifies this as one of only two hero Y-wing miniatures and as the model most famous for leading the first trench run before Vader destroys it.

common_labels: Tiger Sprockets; Alan Ladd Jr Y-Wing
notes: Propstore states this gifted Alan Ladd Jr miniature appears in the close-up form-up shot and is the first ship Vader destroys, but that overlaps awkwardly with Heritage's Gold Leader / TIE Killer description.

common_labels: red stripe blue artoo; ILM Touring Hero Y-Wing
notes: Consistently treated in fan research as an ANH-period finished Y-wing that remained in the archive and later toured, but precise screen identity is unresolved.
Fan research treats this as a detonated pyro and possibly a stand-in for a more finished Y-wing.
Sources distinguish rougher, permanently mounted Y-wing pyros from the high-finish ships; exploded components confirm such pyros existed physically.
A wedge dated 3/6/1976 shows a maroon-painted TIE test hull used for motion blur and compositing evaluation.
Best TIE-specific ANH survey argues that plain hulls with no dark grey accent markings were the norm, which is why ship-by-ship differentiation is difficult.

common_labels: Chronicles TIE; likely Vader wingman TIE
known_identity_states: dark grey accent details; two yellow squares above right cannon
notes: Treated in fan research as a specific ANH TIE with darker-grey details and tiny yellow markings, linked by screencap to one of Vader's wingmen.
Appears to be a distinct ANH damage-state TIE; unclear whether it was a separate build or a redressed standard hull.
Fan research treats this as a real photographed ANH TIE state rather than a kit-only marking option, but the ID is still less secure than the others.
Auction material confirms ANH TIE pyros existed physically and that at least some wings were cast from hero moulds, using materials chosen for dramatic breakup.

known_identity_states: bottom hatches; wing collar; blaster light
anh_status: not a complete variant by itself
notes: Does not prove a separate named TIE, but confirms active casting and assembly of ANH TIE components at ILM.
